ocr_caption:
TREATMENT OF CONTRAST REACTIONS
non allergic-type acute contrast reactions
allergic-type acute contrast reactions
Hypotension and bradycardia (vasovagal)
Urticaria (hives)
1.ECG, pulse oximetry, blood pressure

1. Discontinue injection if not completed.

2. Oxygen via facemask

2. Ifmild, no treatment.

3. Elevate legs or Trendelenburg

3. Ifmoderate:

4. Administer IV fluids
Diphenhydramine PO/IV/IM 25-50 mg
5.Ifp poorly responsive, atropine 0.5 mg IV

4. If severe:
May repeat upt to 3 mg total
Epinephrine IM 1:1,000, 0.3 ml (0.3 mg)

6. Call for assistance (at any point)

5. Call for assistance (at any point)
Pulmonary edema
Facial or laryngeal edema

1. ECG, pulse oximetry, blood pressure

1. ECG, pulse oximetry, blood pressure

2. Oxygen via facemask

2. Oxygen via facemask

3. Elevate torso

3. Epinephrine IM 1:1,000, 0.3 ml (0.3 mg)

4. Furosemide 20-40 mg IV
or

5. Call for assistance (at any point)
Epinephrine IV 1:10,000, 1 ml (0.1 mg)
May repeat up to 1 mg total

4. Call for assistance (at any point)
Seizures
Bronchospasm (expiratory wheezing)
1.ECG, pulse oximetry, blood pressure

1. ECG, pulse oximetry, blood pressure

2. Oxygen via facemask

2. Oxygen via facemask

3. Consider diazepam 5 mg IV

3. Beta-agonist inhaler (Albuterol)
or

4. If poorly responsive:
midazolam 2.5 mg IV
Epinephrine IM 1:1,000, 0.3 ml (0.3 mg)

4. Continue to monitor vitals and pulse oximetry
or

5. Call for assistance (at any point)
Epinephrine IV 1:10,000, 1-3 ml (0.1-0.3 mg)
May repeat up to 1 mg total

5. Call for assistance (at any point)
Hypotension and tachycardia (anaphylaxis)

1. ECG, pulse oximetry, blood pressure

2. Oxygen via facemask

3. Elevate legs or place Trendelenburg

4. IV fluids rapid bolus

5. Epinephrine IM 1:1,000, 0.3 ml (0.3 mg)
or
Epinephrine IV 1:10,000, 1 ml (0.1 mg)
May repeat up to 1 mg total

6. Call for assistance (at any point)
Physics: 1198
